mnemonics.title: Unification Steps
mnemonics.content: UNIFY: 1) Unite constants/functions 2) Find substitutions 3) Verify occurs check 4) Compose substitutions

mnemonics.title: MGU Properties
mnemonics.content: MGU = Most General Unifier: Unique (up to rename), Idempotent, No redundant bindings

acronyms.usage: The most general substitution that unifies two terms; unique up to variable renaming
U=Unify terms, N=No cycles (occurs check), I=Identify substitutions, F=Fail or return, Y=Yes (success)

acronyms.expansion: Unification is the core mechanism for pattern matching
acronyms.usage: U=Uniform replacement, N=No variable in its own term, I=Idempotent substitution, F=Find most general unifier

visualTips.visualization: Think of substitution as a 'find and replace' operation: {X/a} means 'wherever you see X, write a'
Imagine two puzzle pieces (terms) being fitted together - variables are the flexible parts that adapt to make them match
X = f(X) creates an infinite loop like saying 'X is a picture of X' - creates infinite regress
MGU binds only what's necessary, leaving other variables free - like solving equations partially

analogies.analogy: Unification is like matching two DNA strands - variables (A-T, G-C base pairs) adapt to make the strands complementary and identical
Variables are万能keys that can be shaped (bound) to fit any lock (term) to make both patterns identical
MGU is like a general recipe - it specifies only essential ingredients (bindings) while allowing flexibility in what you're cooking (other variables)
